1.0 
 
                                                      Page 4/ 11 
I EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
I.1 Products covered by this document 
SEAICE_GLO_SEAICE_L4_NRT_OBSERVATIONS_011_006: Near Real Time (NRT) sea ice drift obtained 
from Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images as well as NRT daily mosaics regridded to a 10km grid. The 
mosaic product contains derived deformation parameters as well. 
SEAICE_GLO_PHY_L4_MY_011_020: this is the long, reprocessed series of daily NRT mosaics ; this will 
include any late data, i.e. data that originally arrived too late to be included in the daily NRT product. 
The period covered by the reprocessed MY product is equal to the entire Sentinel -1 period starting in 
October 2014, and is updated annually with a one year over lap with the NRT mosaic. Gap -
filling/interpolation is only performed in narrow spaces between image scenes to ensure a seamless 
mosaic of data values. 
I.2 Summary of the results 
The high-resolution Synthetic Aperture Radar ( SAR) sea ice drift product has been compared to high 
quality positions from in -situ drifters (GPS equipped Ice Tethered Profilers – ITPs). Validation has only 
been carried out in the Arctic as there is no high-quality in situ data from the Antarctic at the moment. 
There is nothing that indicates that the test results should not also apply (be indicative for) to the 
Antarctic. 
The results show very good correlation (almost 1.0) when tracking offsets are compared to a validation 
reference (good quality GPS buoy p ositions). Mean error values are very small, which indicates almost 
no bias, and the standard deviation of the differences is low. 
It is worth noting that the numbers are consistently good also for the datasets with smaller numbers of 
data samples. Quality is very good also during summer with only a small increase in standard deviation 
when compared to the reference buoy data. 
The drift product has been enhanced in V4 by including information from the automated monthly 
product validation in the

catalog_entry.summary: This document describes the quality and validation of near real-time sea ice drift products and long-term reprocessed daily mosaics from SAR images, covering both Arctic and Antarctic regions.
